name: TensorBoard On Slurm
summary: Run policy-aware TensorBoard monitors from short Slurm allocations.
description: This skill provides a conservative pattern for serving TensorBoard from Slurm-backed HPC systems, including logdir checks, plan-only defaults, explicit port handling, optional tunnel guidance, and cleanup notes for training monitors.

tools.name: sbatch
tools.notes: Used to run TensorBoard inside a scheduled allocation.

tools.name: python3
tools.notes: Used for log directory checks and optional environment inspection.

tools.name: tensorboard
tools.notes: TensorBoard CLI must be available in the selected Python environment.

tools.name: ssh
tools.notes: Used only when the site requires SSH port forwarding to reach compute-node web services.

inputs.name: logdir
inputs.description: Directory containing TensorBoard event files or framework training logs.

inputs.name: port
inputs.description: Port where TensorBoard should listen inside the Slurm allocation.

inputs.name: host
inputs.description: Bind address for TensorBoard, defaulting to localhost-style access.

inputs.name: site_policy
inputs.description: Local policy confirming whether user-started web monitors and tunnels are allowed on compute nodes.

outputs.name: tensorboard_log
outputs.description: Slurm output showing compute host, logdir, selected port, and TensorBoard startup output.

outputs.name: connection_notes
outputs.description: SSH tunnel or site portal notes for reaching the scheduled TensorBoard monitor.

examples.title: Plan or launch TensorBoard on Slurm
Check TensorBoard, Slurm, and logdir prerequisites
Build an SSH tunnel command when required by the site
Review site policy before starting TensorBoard

tests.description: Validate the manifest and referenced examples.
Check that the logdir helper parses.
After site approval, run the preflight, submit the plan-only job, then set RUN_TENSORBOARD=1 in a short allocation and cancel it when finished.
